questions.text: Select all correct statements about the Women's Quota Law that began in April 2026: (Choose all that apply)

B. It allocates 33% seats to women in legislative assemblies
C. It takes effect immediately upon notification
D. It was first implemented in Maharashtra

questions.text: India approved the first semiconductor Special Economic Zone (SEZ) at Dholera, Gujarat in April 2026.

questions.text: Which international summit was held in Lyon, France in April 2026 focusing on 'One Health' approach?

questions.text: Which Indian athlete won the long jump at the Indian Athletics Series in Ranchi in April 2026?

questions.text: The Indian Navy successfully evacuated 18 ships trapped in the Persian Gulf in April 2026.
questions.explanation: India launched an emergency maritime evacuation plan to rescue 18 ships (4 LPG carriers, 3 LNG carriers, and 11 crude oil tankers) trapped in the Persian Gulf area following improved security conditions.

questions.text: The WTO Ministerial Conference (MC14) in April 2026 saw a significant event related to digital trade. What lapsed for the first time in 26 years?

questions.text: Which Indian painting fetched a record price of Rs 167.2 crore at auction in April 2026?

C. The Story Teller by Amrita Sher-Gil

questions.text: Assertion: The Mumbai Cricket Association (MCA) introduced a domestic player contract system in April 2026. Reason: It was designed to provide systematic financial assistance to upcoming players before they join national teams or IPL auctions.
questions.explanation: MCA proposed an innovative domestic player contract system in 2026 to provide financial support to grassroots cricketers, addressing player attrition and talent pipeline issues in Indian cricket.

questions.text: Scientists confirmed that ice has been accumulating on the Moon for at least 1.5 billion years.
questions.explanation: A NASA Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter study published in Nature Astronomy confirmed that water ice in the Moon's permanently shadowed regions has accumulated steadily over the past 1.5 billion years.

questions.text: Which Central Armed Police Force held its first high-level conference in April 2026 with PM Modi as chairperson?
questions.explanation: India hosted the first high-level conference of Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) with PM Narendra Modi as chairperson, focusing on internal security coordination and emerging threats like left-wing extremism.

questions.text: How many Indian soldiers who died in the Mesopotamia campaign during WWI were added to the Basra Memorial in April 2026?

questions.text: Seven AAP Members of Parliament from the Rajya Sabha joined the BJP in April 2026.
questions.explanation: Raghav Chadha, along with six other AAP members including Sandeep Pathak and Ashok Mittal, resigned from AAP and joined the BJP in April 2026, using the Tenth Schedule protection.

questions.text: Which Indian state government passed the Reorganisation Bill in April 2026 to establish Amaravati as its sole capital?

questions.text: What is MALWAN, which was delivered to the Indian Navy in April 2026?

questions.text: The Jan Samarth Portal integrated over 15 central government schemes and 250+ financial institutions by April 2026.
questions.explanation: The Jan Samarth Portal, launched in 2022, had integrated over 15 central government credit-linked schemes and 250+ financial institutions by April 2026, facilitating loans worth 1.06 lakh crore.

questions.text: In April 2026, which spacecraft completed a lunar mission and set a record for farthest human travel from Earth?
questions.explanation: NASA's Artemis II mission, launched on April 1, 2026, successfully returned to Earth on April 10, 2026, with a record journey of 252,752 miles, surpassing Apollo 13's 1970 record.

questions.text: Which international technology company announced integration with Apple CarPlay for voice-only interaction in April 2026?

questions.text: China launched a new batch of internet satellites as part of its Guowang constellation project in April 2026.

questions.text: Which Indian LPG tanker successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz amid regional tensions in April 2026?

questions.text: Assertion: The Reserve Bank of India eased relief norms for disaster-affected borrowers in April 2026. Reason: Banks could automatically provide relief without requiring borrower applications in notified disaster areas.
questions.explanation: The RBI issued revised guidelines effective July 1, 2026, allowing automatic relief to disaster-affected borrowers without requiring formal applications, while also permitting borrowers to opt out within 135 days.

questions.text: Which scientific study on dung beetles published in April 2026 raised concerns about the impact of light pollution?
options: A. Their ability to navigate using the sun
B. Their ability to use the Milky Way galaxy for night navigation
D. Their life span under artificial lights
questions.explanation: Scientists discovered that with the Milky Way being obscured by artificial light in cities, nocturnal navigators like dung beetles might struggle to survive, throwing ecological balance into question and affecting soil fertilization.

questions.text: Which commission held its 128th meeting in April 2026 to review air pollution enforcement?

questions.text: Which Indian state became the first to fully implement the Uniform Civil Code (UCC)?

questions.text: The Atal Setu is India's longest sea bridge connecting Mumbai to Navi Mumbai.

questions.text: The 'Lakhpati Didi' scheme aims to empower rural women from which organization?

questions.text: Which celestial body is ISRO's Aditya-L1 mission designed to study?

questions.text: What is the designated landing site name of India's Chandrayaan-3 on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near Shiv Shakti Point on the Moon's south polar region.

questions.text: Japan successfully landed the SLIM spacecraft on the lunar surface in June 2026.
questions.explanation: Japan's SLIM (Smart Lander for Investigating Moon) successfully completed a lunar landing mission.

questions.text: ESA's JUICE mission is primarily exploring which planet and its moons?
questions.explanation: The JUICE (Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer) mission by ESA is designed to study Jupiter and its potentially habitable moons.

questions.text: Which country officially hosted the G20 Summit in 2024?
questions.explanation: Brazil hosted the G20 Summit, which brings together the world's largest economies.

questions.text: Which of the following were newly added to the BRICS group in the recent expansion?
questions.explanation: Egypt, Ethiopia, and UAE were added to BRICS, while Argentina did not join during this expansion.

questions.text: Where was the UN Climate Change Conference (COP28) held?
questions.explanation: COP28 was held in Dubai, UAE, where important climate action commitments were made.

questions.text: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
questions.explanation: António Guterres is the current UN Secretary-General, serving since 2017.

questions.text: Who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for her activism against women's oppression in Iran?
questions.explanation: Narges Mohammadi received the Nobel Peace Prize for her courageous fight for human rights in Iran.

questions.text: Which film won the Academy Award for Best Picture in 2024?
options: D. Killers of the Flower Moon
questions.explanation: Oppenheimer won the Best Picture Oscar at the 96th Academy Awards ceremony.

questions.text: M.S. Swaminathan was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna for his role in the Green Revolution.
questions.explanation: M.S. Swaminathan, the father of India's Green Revolution, was honored with the Bharat Ratna posthumously.

questions.text: Which country won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Australia defeated India in the final to win the 2023 ICC Men's Cricket World Cup.

questions.text: Which city was scheduled to host the 2024 Summer Olympics?
questions.explanation: Paris, France hosted the 2024 Summer Olympic Games.

questions.text: Who won the men's singles title at the Australian Open 2024?
questions.explanation: Jannik Sinner won the Australian Open 2024 men's singles championship.

questions.text: Which team won the FIFA Women's World Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Spain defeated England in the final to win the 2023 FIFA Women's World Cup.

questions.text: What is 'Sora', recently unveiled by OpenAI?
questions.explanation: Sora is an AI model developed by OpenAI that can generate realistic videos from text descriptions.

questions.text: Which company developed the Gemini AI model?
questions.explanation: Google developed Gemini, an advanced multimodal AI model.

questions.text: AIRAWAT is India's fastest indigenous AI supercomputer.
questions.explanation: AIRAWAT is India's high-performance computing supercomputer designed specifically for AI and ML research.

questions.text: What was the first name of the human patient to receive a Neuralink brain implant?
questions.explanation: Noland Arbaugh became the first human to receive a successful Neuralink brain implant.

questions.text: At which climate summit was the 'Global Goal on Adaptation' framework adopted?
questions.explanation: The Global Goal on Adaptation was formally adopted at COP28 held in Dubai.

questions.text: Which Indian state recorded the highest number of snow leopards?
questions.explanation: Ladakh has the highest population of snow leopards in India, as per recent surveys.

questions.text: Which city is known as the 'Tiger Capital of India'?
questions.explanation: Nagpur, located in Maharashtra, is known as the Tiger Capital of India due to Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve.

questions.text: Exercise 'Desert Cyclone' is a joint military drill between India and which country?
questions.explanation: Desert Cyclone is a bilateral military exercise between India and the UAE.

questions.text: What is 'Ugram', recently developed by DRDO?
questions.explanation: Ugram is an advanced assault rifle developed by DRDO for the Indian armed forces.

questions.text: Which nation's first indigenous aircraft carrier is INS Vikrant?
options: A. Only aircraft carrier in Asia
C. The largest ship in South Asia
D. Named after a historical warrior
questions.explanation: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier.

questions.text: What is the RBI's digital currency called?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) launched the e-Rupee, India's digital currency.

questions.text: Which country became the 3rd largest economy, overtaking Japan?
questions.explanation: India has grown to become the world's 3rd largest economy by nominal GDP.

questions.text: Which of the following organizations publish economic growth reports?
questions.explanation: The World Bank and IMF regularly publish economic forecasts and reports, including Global Economic Prospects.

questions.text: Which Indian city was named 'City of Music' by UNESCO in the Creative Cities Network?
questions.explanation: Gwalior was recognized by UNESCO as a City of Music, celebrated for its rich classical music heritage.

questions.text: Match the government schemes with their primary beneficiaries:
questions.explanation: Each scheme targets specific beneficiaries: Surya Ghar for solar users, Lakhpati Didi for women, Atal Setu for commuters, and Aditya-L1 for space research.

questions.text: Rohan Bopanna became the oldest world No. 1 player in professional tennis.
questions.explanation: Rohan Bopanna achieved the historic milestone of becoming the world's oldest world No. 1 in men's doubles tennis.

questions.text: Which country launched the world's first 4th-generation nuclear reactor?
questions.explanation: China successfully launched its first 4th-generation nuclear reactor, advancing nuclear technology.

questions.text: India's first semiconductor fab will be established by Tata Group and PSMC in _______.
questions.explanation: The semiconductor manufacturing unit is planned for Gujarat, marking a significant step in India's electronics manufacturing.

questions.text: Which country recently became the 32nd member of NATO?
questions.explanation: Sweden officially joined NATO as its 32nd member, expanding the alliance's reach in Northern Europe.

questions.text: What was the theme of World Environment Day 2024?
options: C. Land restoration, desertification and drought resilience
questions.explanation: The theme focused on combating desertification and promoting land restoration globally.

questions.text: Exercise 'Mitra Shakti' is a bilateral military exercise between India and which country?
questions.explanation: Mitra Shakti is a joint military exercise conducted between India and Sri Lanka.

questions.text: On which dates did the rare red aurora appear over Hanle in Ladakh in January 2026?
questions.explanation: The red aurora appeared over Hanle, Ladakh on the night of January 19-20, 2026, caused by a strong X-class solar flare and coronal mass ejection.

questions.text: What was the cause of the red aurora visible in Ladakh?
options: A. Volcanic eruption in Ladakh region
B. X-class solar flare and coronal mass ejection
questions.explanation: An X-class solar flare on January 18 and a high-speed CME at 1,700 km/s caused the red aurora by exciting oxygen atoms at high altitudes.

questions.text: NITI Aayog launched which countrywide movement to enhance development in underdeveloped regions?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog launched Sampoornata Abhiyan 2.0 in January 2026, a 3-month program to improve development in Aspirational Districts and Blocks.

questions.text: When will India conduct its first-ever Household Income Survey?
questions.explanation: India's first statewide Household Income Survey is scheduled to start in February 2026, led by the Ministry of Statistics and Program Implementation.

questions.text: Which Indian-EU trade agreement was signed on January 27, 2026?
questions.explanation: India and the EU concluded negotiations for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement, known as the 'Mother of All Deals' by trade analysts.

questions.text: What is India's preferential access to EU tariff lines under the new FTA?
questions.explanation: India has gained preferential access to 97% of EU tariff lines, covering 99.5% of trade value under the India-EU FTA.

questions.text: In which year was the 77th Republic Day of India celebrated?
questions.explanation: India celebrated its 77th Republic Day on January 26, 2026, commemorating the adoption of the Indian Constitution in 1950.

questions.text: What was the central theme of the 77th Republic Day parade in 2026?
questions.explanation: The 77th Republic Day 2026 celebrated 150 years of Vande Mataram, the national song's 1876 original composition.

questions.text: Which Union Minister launched the official website and logo for India's BRICS Chairmanship 2026?
options: B. External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar
questions.explanation: External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar launched the BRICS 2026 logo, theme, and website in New Delhi in January 2026.

questions.text: What was the theme of India's BRICS Chairmanship 2026?
options: B. Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability
C. Peace and Security in Asia
questions.explanation: India's BRICS 2026 theme is 'Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability,' reflecting a people-centric approach.

questions.text: Which city hosted the first public Army Day parade outside cantonment areas?
questions.explanation: Jaipur hosted India's first public Army Day parade outside cantonment areas on January 15, 2026, bringing military celebrations to civilian spaces.

questions.text: What is the theme of the 78th Indian Army Day 2026?
options: B. Year of Networking and Data Centricity
questions.explanation: The 78th Army Day theme is 'Year of Networking and Data Centricity,' emphasizing digitalization and modern warfare capabilities.

questions.text: Lucknow became which state's first Zero Fresh Waste Dump City?
questions.explanation: Lucknow achieved the milestone of becoming Uttar Pradesh's first Zero Fresh Waste Dump City by scientifically processing 100% municipal solid waste.

questions.text: Census 2027 Phase I will be conducted between which months?
questions.explanation: Census 2027 Phase I (House Listing and Housing Enumeration) will be conducted from April 1 to September 30, 2026.

questions.text: How many questions are included in Census 2027 Phase I questionnaire?
questions.explanation: The Census 2027 Phase I questionnaire contains 33 questions focusing on housing infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions.

questions.text: What is the new UPI-linked withdrawal facility under EPFO 3.0 expected by?
questions.explanation: The UPI-linked withdrawal facility under EPFO 3.0 is expected to be implemented by April 2026, allowing ATM-like withdrawals.

questions.text: Which Indian organization launched the Responsible Nations Index in January 2026?
questions.explanation: The World Intellectual Foundation launched the Responsible Nations Index at Dr. Ambedkar International Centre, New Delhi.

questions.text: At what rank did India place in the Responsible Nations Index 2026?
questions.explanation: India ranks 16th in the Responsible Nations Index 2026, ahead of the United States (66th) and China (68th).

questions.text: Which state witnessed the Hot Air Balloon Festival in January 2026?
questions.explanation: The Hot Air Balloon Festival was recently started in Telangana, providing a unique recreational and tourism experience.

questions.text: Who was appointed as the new Director General of NIA in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Senior police officer Rakesh Agrawal was appointed as the new Director General of the National Investigation Agency (NIA).

questions.text: Which bank launched India's first solar-powered ATM van?
questions.explanation: Tripura Gramin Bank launched 'TGB On Wheels,' India's first solar-powered mobile ATM van for remote banking services.

questions.text: What is the objective of Tripura Gramin Bank's solar ATM van initiative?
options: A. Generate revenue for the bank
B. Financial inclusion in remote areas
questions.explanation: The solar ATM van aims to provide uninterrupted banking services to remote and electricity-deficient areas, promoting financial inclusion.

questions.text: What was the combined market value of the India-EU FTA announced?
questions.explanation: The India-EU FTA generates a combined market value of USD 24 trillion, covering the 2nd and 4th largest economies.

questions.text: In which year did India and the EU first launch FTA negotiations?
questions.explanation: India and the EU first began FTA negotiations in 2007, which were suspended in 2013 and resumed in 2022 before concluding in January 2026.

questions.text: Which Union Minister launched 2 major bamboo projects in the Northeast?
options: B. Minister of Development of North Eastern Region
questions.explanation: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia (Minister of Development of North Eastern Region) launched 2 bamboo projects worth Rs 82.5 crore in Guwahati.

questions.text: What was the total investment in the two bamboo projects launched in the Northeast?
questions.explanation: The two bamboo projects in the Northeast were worth Rs 82.5 crore, launched on January 26, 2026, in Guwahati.

questions.text: What is the LRASiM missile's striking speed?
questions.explanation: The Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile (LRASiM) travels at Mach 10 (approx. 3.37 km/sec), making it among the fastest missiles globally.

questions.text: What is the maximum range of India's LRASiM missile?
questions.explanation: The LRASiM has a range of 1500 km, allowing the Indian Navy to project power far beyond traditional limits.

questions.text: India became the first country to commercially produce which eco-friendly material?
questions.explanation: India became the first country to produce bio-bitumen commercially, using agricultural byproducts as an eco-friendly substitute for petroleum-based bitumen.

questions.text: What percentage of greenhouse gas reduction does bio-bitumen provide compared to conventional bitumen?
questions.explanation: Bio-bitumen reduces greenhouse gases by 60-70% compared to conventional bitumen, helping address climate change and stubble-burning pollution.

questions.text: Which organization led the development of bio-bitumen technology in India?
questions.explanation: The Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), specifically the Indian Institute of Petroleum, developed the bio-bitumen technology.

questions.text: What is the name of Aadhaar's mascot launched in January 2026?
questions.explanation: The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) launched 'Udai' as the Aadhaar mascot to help clear citizens' doubts regarding Aadhaar-related services.

questions.text: Which state recently announced a comprehensive action plan for cheetah conservation in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh, home to the Kuno National Park, announced a new, enhanced action plan to improve the survival and acclimatization of translocated cheetahs.

questions.text: Which Indian city launched a fully integrated 'Zero Carbon' public transport initiative in early 2026?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru launched a comprehensive zero-carbon public transport initiative in early 2026, integrating fully electric buses and extensive renewable charging stations.
